news.title: Betaalbare biefstuk, flinterdunne marges en 800 gasten per dag: het succesrecept achter Bouillon d’Amsterdam - De Ondernemer
news.summary:
# Summary

Bouillon d'Amsterdam has become a massive success in Amsterdam by serving affordable steak to around 800 customers daily, operating on extremely thin profit margins. The restaurant's business model focuses on high volume and low prices rather than premium positioning, making quality beef accessible to everyday diners. Visitors to Amsterdam would care because it offers an efficient way to enjoy a satisfying steak meal at budget-friendly prices, rather than paying premium prices at traditional steakhouses.

news.title: Antwerps sterrenrestaurant ’t Fornuis sluit na 50 jaar, maar heropent kleinschaliger aan de overkant - HLN
news.summary:
# Summary

Antwerp's acclaimed Michelin-starred restaurant **t Fornuis** is closing after 50 years at its current location, but will reopen in a smaller format across the street. Visitors to Antwerp interested in fine dining should note this transition, as the restaurant will continue operating under a new, more intimate concept rather than disappearing entirely. This is significant for food enthusiasts who value the restaurant's long-standing reputation and refined cuisine.

news.title: Restaurant ’t Fornuis gaat na 50 jaar dicht, chef Johan Segers start aan overkant kleinere zaak met zelfde naam - GVA
news.summary:
# Summary

After 50 years in business, the well-established Belgian restaurant 't Fornuis is closing, but chef Johan Segers is opening a smaller replacement location across the street under the same name. Visitors to the city who appreciated 't Fornuis can expect to find the restaurant's familiar concept in a more compact format at its new location. This transition allows the beloved dining spot to continue operating while adapting to a new scale and space.

news.title: Iconisch restaurant ’t Fornuis sluit na halve eeuw de deuren: ‘Mijn leven begint opnieuw’ - De Morgen
news.summary:
# Summary

After 50 years of operation, the iconic Belgian restaurant 't Fornuis has permanently closed its doors. The closure marks the end of an era for this beloved establishment that has been a dining fixture in the community for decades. Visitors to the city looking for classic, long-standing dining experiences will need to seek alternatives, as this landmark restaurant is no longer available.
